skill.name: verify-output
skill.description: Guides agents to validate structured JSON outputs against known schemas before writing final files.
skill.summary: Validate agent output files against required schemas before completion.
skill.author: Clouder0

security_audit.summary: The static Ruby backtick findings are mostly false positives from Markdown code fences and inline code, not Ruby execution. The confirmed concern is that the skill instructs agents to run Bash commands and move files, which is legitimate for validation but requires path discipline. Weak cryptography and system reconnaissance detections are false positives, and no prompt injection, exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Bash Validation Commands Require Careful Path Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to run a local validation script and move temporary output files into final paths. This is appropriate for schema validation, but unsafe schema names or file paths could affect unintended files if an agent expands untrusted input into these commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash commands are explicitly documented and the skill allows Bash. The commands appear legitimate, so the risk is operational misuse rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited lines contain the skill description or schema names, not cryptographic algorithms or security-sensitive hashing. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are plain documentation and schema references. There is no code path or algorithm invocation related to cryptography.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The cited lines describe validation errors, enum values, common mistakes, and validation principles. No evidence found of host discovery, process listing, network scanning, or environment enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scanner matched words inside validation guidance rather than reconnaissance commands. The surrounding context is documentation for schema correctness.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Shell Commands in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell command examples for running a validator and moving output files. These commands should be executed only against repository-local, expected paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present in Markdown command examples. The commands are not malicious by themselves, but they create a real external command risk factor.

content.value_statement: Structured agent outputs often fail because required fields or enum values are missing. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a repeatable validation flow before final files are written.
actual_capabilities: Maps common agent roles to the expected output schema.
Directs validation through a local scripts/validate.sh command.
Uses temporary output files before moving validated files into place.
Lists common schema validation errors and practical fixes.
Defines compact JSON expectations for agent output files.
Recommends retrying validation before reporting a failure.
limitations: Requires a compatible local scripts/validate.sh file.
Covers only the schemas named in the skill documentation.
Does not verify business logic or factual correctness.
Requires careful handling of file paths before running Bash commands.

use_cases.title: Pre-submit Output Checks
use_cases.description: Validate a generated task output file before handing it to another workflow stage.

use_cases.title: Schema Compliance Reviews
use_cases.description: Confirm that required fields, enum values, and nested structures match the expected schema.

use_cases.title: Reliable Agent Pipelines
use_cases.description: Reduce downstream failures by validating intermediate JSON reports before final writes.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Basic Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the verify-output skill to check my generated output against the correct schema before finalizing it.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner validation before saving a file

prompt_templates.title: Fix Schema Errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate this task output, explain any schema errors in plain language, fix the object, and validate again.
prompt_templates.scenario: Correcting required fields and type mismatches

prompt_templates.title: Validate Role-specific Reports
prompt_templates.prompt: Determine the correct schema for this agent role, write the report to a temporary path, validate it, and only then move it to the final path.
prompt_templates.scenario: Agent workflow with role-based schema selection

prompt_templates.title: Harden a Validation Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this multi-agent output process, apply the pre-write validation pattern, and identify where schema validation should block completion.

output_examples.input: An implementer agent has produced a task result and needs to finalize it.
output_examples.output: The agent selects the task-output schema, validates a temporary file, fixes any missing fields, and then writes the final output.

output_examples.input: A verifier sees an error that a field has the wrong type.
output_examples.output: The verifier explains the mismatch, changes the value to the expected structure, and repeats validation before completion.

output_examples.input: A workflow owner wants fewer malformed reports in memory folders.
output_examples.output: The workflow adds pre-write validation for each report type and treats repeated validation failure as a blocking issue.

best_practices: Validate files at a temporary path before writing the final output.
Use only known schema names and expected repository-local paths.
Read validation errors carefully and retry after each focused correction.
anti_patterns: Writing final output before schema validation passes.
Passing unreviewed user-controlled paths into Bash commands.
Ignoring missing required fields because the output looks readable.

faq.question: What does this skill validate?
faq.answer: It validates structured agent output files against named local schemas before final writes.

faq.question: Does it create new schemas?
faq.answer: No. It assumes the expected schemas and validation script already exist in the repository.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Why does it use temporary files?
faq.answer: Temporary files prevent invalid output from replacing a final report before validation passes.

faq.question: Is there a security concern?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses Bash commands, so agents should use only trusted schema names and safe local paths.

faq.question: What happens when validation fails?
faq.answer: The skill recommends reading the error, fixing the output, and retrying before reporting failure.
